name: golang-github-jacobsa-reqtrace-dev
title: golang-github-jacobsa-reqtrace-dev
description:
simple request tracing
reqtrace is a package for simple request tracing. It requires nothing of its
user except:
.
* They must use golang.org/x/net/context.
* They must add a single line to each function they want to be visible in
traces.
.
In particular, reqtrace is console-based and doesn't require an HTTP server.
.
Warning: This package is still barebones and in its early days. The author
reserves the right to make backwards-incompatible changes to its API. But if
it's useful to you in your current form, have at it.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use golang-github-jacobsa-reqtrace-dev because simple request tracing.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use golang-github-jacobsa-reqtrace-dev as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair golang-github-jacobsa-reqtrace-dev with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `golang-github-jacobsa-reqtrace-dev --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
